title: Croydonites Fringe: Mimi (WIP)
description:
Rosie Yadid – cabaret queen, burlesque clown and “power packing chanteuse” – presents the high-flying antics of a vaudeville legend who won’t be forgotten.

Chayim Solonely looks back on the life of his beloved wife, whose indomitable spirit dominates her funeral for one last chance to serve sass and shake ass.

This is part of the second edition of the Croydonites Fringe, presented in collaboration with Stanley Arts and funded by Croydon Council and Arts Council England.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Croydonites Fringe: Emmy Fyles: Not Like That, You’re Doing It Wrong
description:
Join Emmy Fyles (BBC3, Comedy Central, LADbible) as she serves up her “multimedia extravaganza” (PowerPoint show she made on her laptop) exploring why things are a dystopian-level bin fire for women in 2026.

(But, like, in a totally funny, cute way…)

This is part of the second edition of the Croydonites Fringe, presented in collaboration with Stanley Arts and funded by Croydon Council and Arts Council England.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Hartbeeps
description:
Hartbeeps Magical Classes for Babies and Toddlers

Location: Society Room

Date: Jul 2

Time: 10:00 am till 1:30 pm

Tickets: £14 drop in, £7 sibling. £11/week when you book the term, rolling so you can join at any time.

Ages: 8 weeks - 5 years old

The original musical story tellers and creators of multi-sensory play for babies and young children, you have arrived in the right place and are in great hands.

Step inside, take a wander around and have a magical time!

Timetable:

10am Happy House (for toddlers and their younger siblings)

11:15 Baby Beeps (for babies that are sitting confidently or crawling)

12:30 Baby Bells (from newborn to sitting)

Join Dexter Cat & Adrian Mouse in the Hartbeeps Happy House from 10am. Participants take a different sound journey every week, as we set the scene and your Little Hartbeepers build their story! It’s fun fun fun all the way, with our own original versions of classic nursery rhymes, the most fabulous songs, gorgeous puppet characters, dress-up, props and more…

All aboard the Baby Beeps Babble Express, next stop Babble Station! Sessions offer BIG fun in full colour, with larger than life puppet characters, rhymes, raps, claps and rhythmic beats; supporting baby’s blossoming speech and language development.

Baby Bells classes are designed to ignite and delight those freshly acquired senses and help baby to uncurl and release trapped energy. Sessions focus on bonding and building confidence.

Baby Beeps, for babies from 7 months approx. (for babies that are sitting unaided) Baby Bells, for babies from 8 weeks approx. (younger babies very welcome) Hartbeeps are currently running a promotion for babies under 8 weeks to join us for free in our Baby Bells class at 12:30, using the promo code UNDER8WEEKS to claim.

If you have any access requirements, please give us a call on 020 8251 0184 or email us on bookings@stanleyarts.org and we will be happy to help!

Classes are held in spaces throughout Stanley Arts. Details in confirmation email.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Croydonites Fringe: Fitzroy – Palace Boy
description:
Fitzroy! A show ‘disguised as a lecture’ about the Captain of The Beagle – the ship that took Darwin to The Galápagos Islands. It’s a light, educational comedy show exploring class, empire, race and racism, mental health, love, jealousy and the weather.

This is part of the second edition of the Croydonites Fringe, presented in collaboration with Stanley Arts and funded by Croydon Council and Arts Council England.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Croydonites Fringe: Hero
description:
The Hero with a Thousand Faces is an evocative dance work by Jose Agudo | Agudo Dance Company, inspired by Joseph Campbell’s concept of the monomyth and the universal journey of the hero.

Merging the rhythmic precision of Kathak, the fiery passion of flamenco, and the fluidity of contemporary dance, Agudo crafts a multi-layered exploration of a personal odyssey from West to East.

This is part of the second edition of the Croydonites Fringe, presented in collaboration with Stanley Arts and funded by Croydon Council and Arts Council England.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Croydonites Fringe: Un-Muted
description:
How do you tell your story when silence is law?

Un-Muted is a powerful solo performance that dives into the silence of legal spaces where voices are erased. Created from Mika Hockman’s groundbreaking research, this evocative piece weaves fractured language, haunting imagery, and untold truths about custody, alienation, and the invisible wounds left by silence. Don’t miss the chance to hear the voiceless speak.

This is a work of fiction created through composite research and performance; any resemblance to real persons or situations is coincidental.

Trigger warning: contains domestic violence content.

This is part of the second edition of the Croydonites Fringe, presented in collaboration with Stanley Arts and funded by Croydon Council and Arts Council England.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: Cherrystars Performing Arts
description:
Cherrystars are an award winning, community performing arts school. Their team consist of BRIT School alumni and professional television and theatre actors who specialise in teaching innovative performing arts classes to the early years.

Star in their magical movies and stage shows building confidence, creativity and community!

The core values at Cherrystars are Communication, Creativity, Confidence Building and Community. They have taken great pride over the last decade, to have provided a safe, nurturing and affirming place in the local community for children to unleash their creativity and develop a love of the arts.

Visit the Cherrystars website today.

Follow Cherrystars on Instagram, Facebook & X (FKA Twitter) for the latest news.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ

title: The Stanley Arts Community Assembly 2026
description:
Are you feeling the impacts of climate change, biodiversity loss and social inequality?

Are you a local citizen wanting to make change for a better future?

Join us for a day-long gathering in the main hall at Stanley Arts to meet others of all ages living and working in Croydon and neighbouring boroughs to share local concerns, visions and pathways of change for our collective wellbeing.

Take part in conversation and creative activity, and connect and build collaborations with people taking action.

We are delighted to be joined by Croydon Council Cabinet Member for Parks and Culture, Councillor Robert Ward who will share updates on the progress being made on carbon reduction, heat, flooding, and air and water pollution, and how the Council is working with residents to build climate and environmental resilience.

Other guest speakers include:

  • Leonie Osborne from Croydon Climate Action,
  • Amanda McGrath from Good Food Matters,
  • Tim Coombe from Croydon Community Energy
  • Matthew Goldschmied on home upgrades to tackle fuel poverty, overheating and for green jobs
  • Tracey Hague from the Clean Air campaign
  • Becky Vicars from the Water Watch campaign
  • Andrea Perry from the Green Streets campaign

These local campaigners will address air and water pollution, biodiversity, climate resilience and energy-efficiency. There will be opportunities to connect with other local action groups including Sustainable Thornton Heath, Croydon Greenpeace and Croydon Cyclists.

The day will include spoken word from local poet Wayne Samuel, the card game ‘Stronger Together’ by Blue Heart and Mod, free vegetarian and vegan lunch, and will end with a networking opportunity in our bar.

Together we’ll leave ready to create collective resilience for the future.

Tickets are free, with an option to make a donation to further our community engagement programming.

If you would find it helpful to have BSL interpretation or other access support at this event, please reach out to kay@stanleyarts.org and we will arrange this for you.
address: Stanley Arts, 12 Stanley Road, South Norwood, London, SE25 4RJ
